question.title: How do I make a one layout go to another layout when an image button is clicked in android?
question.body:
I have a program that's supposed to go from one layout to another when an image button is clicked. Here is my MainActivity code:

public class MainActivity extends Activity {
    private ImageButton abt;

    @Override
    protected void onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState);
        setContentView(R.layout.activity_main);
        abt = (ImageButton) findViewById(R.id.about);

        abt.setOnClickListener(new OnClickListener() {
            @Override
            public void onClick(View v) {
                Intent intent = new Intent(MainActivity.this, AboutActivity.class);
                 startActivity(intent);
            }
        });
    }

    @Override
    public boolean onCreateOptionsMenu(Menu menu) {
        getMenuInflater().inflate(R.menu.main, menu);
        return true;
    }

    @Override
    public boolean onOptionsItemSelected(MenuItem item) {
        int id = item.getItemId();
        if (id == R.id.action_settings) {
            return true;
        }
        return super.onOptionsItemSelected(item);
    }
}

And here is my AboutActivity:

   public class AboutActivity extends Activity{
        private ImageButton aboutbk;
        @Override
        protected void onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState) {
            super.onCreate(savedInstanceState);
            setContentView(R.layout.activity_about);
            aboutbk = (ImageButton) findViewById(R.id.aboutBack);

            aboutbk.setOnClickListener(new OnClickListener() {
                @Override
                public void onClick(View v) {
                    finish();
                }
            });
        }

        @Override
        public boolean onCreateOptionsMenu(Menu menu) {
            return super.onCreateOptionsMenu(menu);
        }

        @Override
        public boolean onOptionsItemSelected(MenuItem item) {
            return super.onOptionsItemSelected(item);
        }

My activity_main xml layout file: (I already finished the layout here for my actual program but right now I'm just using my About image button)

<LinearLayout xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    android:layout_width="fill_parent"
    android:layout_height="fill_parent"
    android:background="@drawable/background"
    android:orientation="horizontal" >

    <LinearLayout
        android:layout_width="wrap_content"
        android:layout_height="wrap_content" >

        <ImageView
            android:id="@+id/imageView1"
            android:layout_width="192dp"
            android:layout_height="600dp"
            android:layout_marginLeft="20dp"
            android:src="@drawable/welcome" />
    </LinearLayout>

    <LinearLayout
        android:layout_width="288dp"
        android:layout_height="593dp"
        android:orientation="vertical"
        android:paddingLeft="25dp"
        android:paddingTop="15dp" >

        <ImageView
            android:id="@+id/imageView2"
            android:layout_width="140dp"
            android:layout_height="158dp"
            android:src="@drawable/view" />

        <ImageButton
            android:id="@+id/letter"
            android:layout_width="match_parent"
            android:layout_height="43dp"
            android:background="@android:color/transparent"
            android:src="@drawable/letter" />

        <ImageButton
            android:id="@+id/topic"
            android:layout_width="match_parent"
            android:layout_height="43dp"
            android:layout_marginTop="5dp"
            android:background="@android:color/transparent"
            android:src="@drawable/topic" />


        <ImageButton
            android:id="@+id/about"
            android:layout_width="match_parent"
            android:layout_height="60dp"
            android:layout_gravity="bottom"
            android:layout_marginTop="25dp"
            android:background="@android:color/transparent"
            android:paddingTop="10dp"
            android:src="@drawable/buttonabout" />

        <ImageView
            android:id="@+id/imageView3"
            android:layout_width="73dp"
            android:layout_height="78dp"
            android:layout_marginTop="20dp"
            android:src="@drawable/search" />

        <EditText
            android:id="@+id/wrdsearch"
            android:layout_width="200dp"
            android:layout_height="wrap_content"
            android:hint="search" />

        <ImageButton
            android:id="@+id/searchgo"
            android:layout_width="match_parent"
            android:layout_height="wrap_content"
            android:layout_marginLeft="40dp"
            android:layout_marginTop="15dp"
            android:background="@android:color/transparent"
            android:src="@drawable/go" />
    </LinearLayout>

</LinearLayout>

My activity_about xml layout file:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<LinearLayout xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    android:layout_width="match_parent"
    android:layout_height="match_parent"
    android:orientation="horizontal" >

    <ImageButton
        android:id="@+id/aboutBack"
        android:layout_width="515dp"
        android:layout_height="630dp"
        android:background="@drawable/aboutbg"
        android:src="@drawable/about2" />

</LinearLayout>

My logcat errors:

This is pretty simple but it won't work? I already declared the second activity on my AndroidManifest but it still won't work? Please help me I've been googling around trying to find a solution but I can't seem to find one.
